concept_name: Combination of Resistors — Series Adds Resistance, Parallel Opens New Paths
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.3 Current Electricity (§3.10 Combination of Resistors — Series and Parallel) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. DC Pandey consulted for chapter SCOPE only (Current Electricity ToC: Ohm's law / resistivity / combination-of-resistors sections) to confirm atomic boundaries — no teaching method, example problem, or figure imported. HC Verma consulted for teaching SEQUENCE (open on a one-resistor baseline loop and let the ammeter settle at one ampere; splice a second resistor in-line and watch every electron in the loop slow together as resistances add; confirm the far-side ammeters never lag — current is never used up, only voltage drops; sweep a potential probe around the loop to show the voltage staircasing down twice; lift that same resistor out and re-land it ACROSS the first, the PRIMARY aha as the needle LEAPS rather than falls; grow the second resistor and watch the current split unevenly at the junction by ease of path, never by half; collapse the two branches into their single equivalent box below the smallest resistor; open a branch switch to show parallel branches are independent — the anchor payoff for why every home is wired this way; then hand over every dial), never copied. The atomic idea: in SERIES the same current threads every resistor and voltages add — R_eq = R1 + R2 + …; in PARALLEL every branch shares the same voltage and currents add at the junction — 1/R_eq = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + … — so the parallel equivalent resistance is always LESS than the smallest branch alone. SCOPE: this is the FOURTH concept of Ch.3, continuous with drift_velocity (SHIPPED, opener), ohms_law (SHIPPED, second), and resistivity (SHIPPED, third) — same particle_field (2D p5.js) renderer family, this time a dedicated circuit scenario (battery + wire loop + resistor boxes + junction + ammeters) rather than the free-drift gas. Does NOT cover EMF and internal resistance (deferred to cells_emf_internal_resistance), combinations of cells (deferred to combination_of_cells), Kirchhoff's rules (deferred to kirchhoffs_laws), Wheatstone/metre bridge (deferred), power/heating in combinations (deferred to electric_power_heating), or multi-step mixed-network reduction drills (the two combination laws taught here ARE the reduction tool; chaining them belongs to the Kirchhoff/Wheatstone concepts). Renderer-reality note: this concept does NOT drive R_eq/i/i1/i2 via physics_engine_config.formulas/PM_interpolate — particle_field_renderer.ts computes them natively in engine JS via the shipped circuit adapter (cVolt/cR1/cR2/cTopology/cSwitchOpen/cCurrents), reading slider_controls.{V,R1,R2,topology,switch}; physics_engine_config below is authored as documentation per the Zod requirement. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the three genuine misconceptions — 'current is used up crossing a resistor', 'adding any resistor increases total resistance', and 'parallel branches share current equally' — are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + straightforward contrast-in-motion beats at STATE_3/STATE_5/STATE_6, no predict-pause). Calibration note: unlike resistivity (which needs a hidden fixed V_supply to make real copper's milliohm-scale resistance drive a legible demo meter), this concept treats R1/R2 as discrete component-scale resistors (Ω-scale by construction, like real resistor decade boxes) driven by a normal battery-scale voltage (0–12 V) — i = V/R_eq lands directly in a legible 0.05–12 A range with zero hidden calibration constant.

real_world_anchor.primary: Every appliance in an Indian home — the ceiling fan, the fridge, the TV, the geyser — hangs on its own parallel branch across the same 220-volt mains. That is why each one gets the full mains voltage, why the fridge doesn't blink when you switch the fan off, and why the meter reading climbs as you switch more things ON: every new appliance is a new parallel path, and the house's total resistance FALLS.
real_world_anchor.secondary: The old style of decorative string lights wires dozens of tiny bulbs in SERIES — one bulb dies and the whole string goes dark, and someone has to test bulb by bulb to find the culprit. Modern strings (and homes) wire in parallel precisely to escape this: one broken filament breaks only its own path.

V.name: Battery voltage
V.role: slider — S9 only; locked to 6 via variable_overrides on S1–S8, never adjustable until explore

R1.name: Resistor 1
R1.role: slider — S9 only; locked to 6 on S1–S8 — R1 never moves in any guided state, the fixed reference every other change is measured against

R2.name: Resistor 2
R2.role: slider — live on S2/S4/S6/S7/S9; locked to 6 on S1/S3/S5/S8; auto-sweeps 6→12 on the state clock in S6/S7

topology.name: Circuit topology (0=series, 1=parallel)
topology.role: slider (2-state toggle) — live ONLY in S9; every guided state drives it via the state's own topology flag, never a direct drag; rendered as text (Series/Parallel), never raw 0/1

switch.name: Branch-2 switch (0=open, 1=closed)
switch.role: slider (2-state toggle) — live ONLY in S8/S9; the switch element does not exist on screen before S8; rendered as text (Open/Closed), never raw 0/1

R_eq.name: Equivalent resistance
R_eq.role: live readout — the S2 overlay (series) and S7 req_box (parallel)

i_total.name: Total (battery) current
i_total.role: live readout — the main ammeter every state reads

i1.name: Branch 1 / series current
i1.role: live readout — in series equals i_total exactly (S3's matched ammeters); in parallel is R1's own branch current (S6/S8)

i2.name: Branch 2 current (switch-gated)
i2.role: live readout — drives branch-2 bead speed; exactly 0 the instant the switch opens (S8)

V1.name: Voltage across R1 (series)
V1.role: live readout — S4's first staircase step

V2.name: Voltage across R2 (series)
V2.role: live readout — S4's second staircase step

V_branch.name: Voltage across either parallel branch
V_branch.role: live readout — S6's 'both branches read 6.0 V'

formulas.current_division_ratio: in parallel mode, i1 / i2 = R2 / R1 — the inverse-resistance ratio, exact at every (R1,R2)

constraints: i_total = V / R_eq exactly at every (V,R1,R2,topology) combination — Ohm's law applied to the equivalent resistance
Series: R_eq = R1 + R2 ALWAYS ≥ max(R1,R2) — adding a series resistor never lowers R_eq or raises current
Parallel: 1/R_eq = 1/R1 + 1/R2, so R_eq ALWAYS < min(R1,R2) — adding a parallel branch never raises R_eq; as R2 → ∞, R_eq → R1 from below, never reaching or exceeding it
Junction current conservation (KCL): i_total = i1 + i2 at every instant in parallel mode; series mode has i uniform everywhere
Current-division ratio: i1/i2 = R2/R1 in parallel mode — the INVERSE resistance ratio, bigger resistor gets LESS current
Series voltage division: V1 + V2 = V always, with V1/V2 = R1/R2 — the DIRECT resistance ratio, bigger resistor takes the BIGGER voltage share
Both parallel branches see the identical voltage V, independent of the other branch's resistance value
All currents ≥ 0 always; switch = 0 forces the gated current to exactly 0, never a decaying artifact
Every derived quantity (R_eq, i_total, i1, i2, V1, V2) is a pure function of (V,R1,R2,topology,switch) with no hidden internal state
No hidden calibration constant — R1/R2 are component-scale resistors on a battery-scale supply, unlike resistivity's hidden V_supply fudge

aha_moment.statement: Adding a resistor ACROSS instead of in-line makes the current climb, not fall.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The main ammeter leaps from 1.00 A to 2.00 A the instant R2 lands across R1, while the R_eq readout drops to 3 Ω.

STATE_1.title: Baseline: one loop, one resistor — the ammeter settles at one ampere

scene_composition.text: Six volts, one resistor of six ohms — the simplest possible loop.
The ammeter climbs from zero and settles at one ampere.
One ampere — i = V/R1 — is the reference for every state ahead.

tts_sentences.text_en: Six volts from the battery push current through one resistor of six ohms.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Battery से छह volts, छह ohms के एक resistor से होकर current को आगे धकेलते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Battery ఇచ్చే ఆరు volts, ఆరు ohms ఉన్న ఒక resistor గుండా current ని ముందుకు నెడతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the ammeter needle climb from zero and settle at one ampere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए, ammeter की needle zero से ऊपर चढ़ती है और एक ampere पर टिक जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి, ammeter యొక్క needle zero నుండి పైకి ఎక్కి ఒక ampere వద్ద స్థిరపడుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: One ampere — current I equals voltage V over resistance R — is our reference for every state ahead.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक ampere — current I equals voltage V over resistance R — आगे आने वाली हर state के लिए हमारा reference है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక ampere — current I equals voltage V over resistance R — ఇక ముందు వచ్చే ప్రతి state కి మన reference.

STATE_2.title: Series build: R2 splices in, resistances add

scene_composition.text: A second resistor, R2, slides in line and splices into the loop.
Every electron in the loop slows together — the ammeter sinks to half.
Resistances simply add: R_eq = R1 + R2 = twelve ohms.

tts_sentences.text_en: A second resistor, R two, slides in line and splices into the loop.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक दूसरा resistor, R two, लाइन में सरककर loop में जुड़ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండో resistor, R two, లైన్‌లో జారి loop లోకి చేరుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: After a beat, every electron everywhere in the loop slows together, and the needle sinks from one ampere to zero point five.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक पल बाद, loop में हर जगह हर electron एक साथ धीमा हो जाता है, और needle एक ampere से गिरकर शून्य दशमलव पाँच पर आ जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కొద్దిసేపటికి, loop అంతటా ప్రతి electron కలిసి నెమ్మదిస్తుంది, మరియు needle ఒక ampere నుండి సున్నా దశాంశం ఐదు కి పడిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Resistances simply add in series: the equivalent resistance, R eq, equals R one plus R two — twelve ohms here.
tts_sentences.text_hi: series में resistances बस जुड़ जाते हैं: equivalent resistance, R eq, equals R one plus R two — यहाँ बारह ohms।
tts_sentences.text_te: series లో resistances కేవలం కూడతాయి: equivalent resistance, R eq, equals R one plus R two — ఇక్కడ పన్నెండు ohms.

STATE_3.title: Same current everywhere in series — nothing is used up

scene_composition.text: Three ammeters watch this loop — before R1, between the two, and after R2.
If current were used up crossing a resistor, the later meters should read less.
All three stay pinned at 0.50 A the whole time — resistors spend voltage, never current.

tts_sentences.text_en: Three ammeters watch this loop: before the first resistor, between the two, and after the second.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीन ammeters इस loop को देख रहे हैं: पहले resistor से पहले, दोनों के बीच में, और दूसरे के बाद।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడు ammeters ఈ loop ని గమనిస్తున్నాయి: మొదటి resistor కి ముందు, రెండింటి మధ్య, మరియు రెండో దాని తర్వాత.

tts_sentences.text_en: If current I were used up crossing a resistor, later meters should read less — watch all three.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अगर current I किसी resistor को पार करते हुए खर्च हो जाता, तो बाद के meters कम दिखाते — तीनों को देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకవేళ current I ఒక resistor ని దాటేటప్పుడు ఖర్చు అయిపోతే, తర్వాతి meters తక్కువ చూపించాలి — మూడింటినీ గమనించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: All three stay pinned at zero point five ampere the whole time: resistors spend voltage V, never current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तीनों पूरे समय शून्य दशमलव पाँच ampere पर टिके रहते हैं: resistors voltage V खर्च करते हैं, current I कभी नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మూడూ ఎప్పుడూ సున్నా దశాంశం ఐదు ampere వద్దే నిలిచి ఉంటాయి: resistors voltage V ని ఖర్చు చేస్తాయి, current I ని ఎప్పుడూ కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Current gets used up crossing a resistor — the resistor after the first one gets less.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Three in-line ammeters — before R1, between the two resistors, and after R2 — stay matched at 0.50 A for the entire dwell, with live per-second crossing tallies.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Resistors spend voltage, never current — in one series loop every plane passes the same electrons per second.

STATE_4.title: Series voltages add — V1 + V2 = V

scene_composition.text: A glowing probe rides the loop, reading the potential as it goes.
Three volts vanish crossing R1, three more crossing R2.
V1 + V2 = V — the bigger resistance always takes the bigger share.

tts_sentences.text_en: A glowing probe rides the loop: three volts vanish crossing R one, three more crossing R two — voltage V one plus voltage V two equals six volts.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक चमकता हुआ probe loop पर चलता है: R one को पार करते ही तीन volts गायब हो जाते हैं, R two को पार करते ही तीन volts और गायब हो जाते हैं — voltage V one plus voltage V two equals छह volts।
tts_sentences.text_te: మెరుస్తున్న ఒక probe loop మీద తిరుగుతుంది: R one ని దాటగానే మూడు volts మాయమవుతాయి, R two ని దాటగానే మరో మూడు volts మాయమవుతాయి — voltage V one plus voltage V two equals ఆరు volts.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag R two to twelve ohms and the steps reshape live — two volts, then four: the bigger resistance always takes the bigger voltage share.
tts_sentences.text_hi: R two को बारह ohms तक खींचिए और steps तुरंत बदल जाते हैं — पहले दो volts, फिर चार: बड़ा resistance हमेशा voltage का बड़ा हिस्सा लेता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: R two ని పన్నెండు ohms వరకు లాగండి, steps వెంటనే మారిపోతాయి — ముందు రెండు volts, తర్వాత నాలుగు: పెద్ద resistance ఎప్పుడూ voltage లో పెద్ద వాటా తీసుకుంటుంది.

STATE_5.title: PRIMARY aha — a parallel path lowers resistance, the ammeter leaps

scene_composition.text: R2 lifts out of line — the loop heals, the needle returns to one ampere.
That same resistor lands ACROSS R1 — a junction blooms.
The needle LEAPS to two amperes — a new path means room for more current, not more obstacle.

tts_sentences.text_en: R two lifts out of line — the loop heals, needle back to one ampere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: R two लाइन से बाहर निकल जाता है — loop फिर से जुड़ जाता है, needle एक ampere पर वापस आ जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: R two లైన్ నుండి బయటకు వస్తుంది — loop మళ్ళీ కుదురుకుంటుంది, needle ఒక ampere కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That same resistor lands ACROSS R one — a junction blooms, and the needle LEAPS to two amperes.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही resistor R one के ACROSS जाकर बैठ जाता है — एक junction बन जाता है, और needle दो amperes तक उछल जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే resistor R one కి ACROSS గా వచ్చి కూర్చుంటుంది — ఒక junction ఏర్పడుతుంది, మరియు needle రెండు amperes కి ఎగిరిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A new path means room for more current, not more obstacle — resistance drops to three ohms, below either resistor alone.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक नया path मतलब और current के लिए जगह, न कि और रुकावट — resistance गिरकर तीन ohms हो जाता है, जो दोनों resistors से अकेले भी कम है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కొత్త path అంటే మరింత current కి చోటు, మరింత అడ్డంకి కాదు — resistance పడిపోయి మూడు ohms అవుతుంది, ఇది ఒక్కో resistor కంటే కూడా తక్కువ.

misconception_watch.belief: Adding ANY resistor increases total resistance and decreases current.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The identical R2 that just sank the needle from 1.00 A to 0.50 A in series now lands ACROSS R1 instead, and the same ammeter LEAPS to 2.00 A while R_eq drops to 3 Ω.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In-line adds an obstacle; across adds a PATH — more paths, more current, less resistance.

STATE_6.title: Current divides by ease of path — not by half

scene_composition.text: R2 grows from six ohms toward twelve — watch the stream split at the junction.
Branch one stays pinned at one ampere; branch two thins to half.
Both branches read the full six volts — they share voltage equally, never current.

tts_sentences.text_en: R two grows from six ohms to twelve — watch the stream split at junction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: R two छह ohms से बढ़कर बारह हो जाता है — देखिए धारा junction पर कैसे बँट जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: R two ఆరు ohms నుండి పన్నెండు కి పెరుగుతుంది — junction వద్ద ప్రవాహం ఎలా చీలిపోతుందో గమనించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Branch one stays pinned at one ampere; branch two thins to zero point five ampere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Branch one एक ampere पर टिका रहता है; branch two घटकर शून्य दशमलव पाँच ampere हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Branch one ఒక ampere వద్దే నిలిచి ఉంటుంది; branch two తగ్గి సున్నా దశాంశం ఐదు ampere అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both branches read the full six volts — they share voltage equally, never current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों branches पूरे छह volts दिखाते हैं — वे voltage को बराबर बाँटते हैं, current को कभी नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు branches పూర్తి ఆరు volts చూపిస్తాయి — అవి voltage ని సమానంగా పంచుకుంటాయి, current ని ఎప్పుడూ కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Current I splits by ease of path — not by half.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current I path की आसानी के हिसाब से बँटता है — आधा-आधा नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current I path యొక్క సౌలభ్యాన్ని బట్టి చీలుతుంది — సగం సగం కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Parallel branches share the current equally.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: With R2 grown to twelve ohms the stream visibly splits 2:1 — 1.00 A down the six-ohm branch, 0.50 A down the twelve-ohm branch — while both branches stay labelled the same 6.0 V.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Branches share VOLTAGE equally, never current — each branch draws i = V/R for itself.

STATE_7.title: 1/R_eq = 1/R1 + 1/R2 — parallel R_eq is below the smallest branch

scene_composition.text: Branch one at six ohms, branch two at twelve — about to collapse into one.
One over R_eq equals one over R1 plus one over R2.
That box reads four ohms — less than the smallest branch — while the total ammeter holds steady.

tts_sentences.text_en: Branch one at six ohms, branch two at twelve, about to collapse into one box.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Branch one छह ohms पर, branch two बारह पर — अभी एक ही box में सिमटने वाले हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Branch one ఆరు ohms వద్ద, branch two పన్నెండు వద్ద — ఇప్పుడు ఒకే box లోకి కుదించబోతున్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch them merge: one over R eq equals one over R one plus one over R two.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए वे कैसे मिलते हैं: एक over R eq equals एक over R one plus एक over R two।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవి ఎలా కలుస్తాయో చూడండి: ఒక over R eq equals ఒక over R one plus ఒక over R two.

tts_sentences.text_en: That box reads four ohms — less than the smallest branch — while the ammeter holds steady at one point five amperes.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह box चार ohms दिखाता है — सबसे छोटे branch से भी कम — जबकि ammeter एक दशमलव पाँच amperes पर स्थिर रहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ box నాలుగు ohms చూపిస్తుంది — అతి చిన్న branch కంటే కూడా తక్కువ — ammeter మాత్రం ఒక దశాంశం ఐదు amperes వద్ద స్థిరంగా ఉంటుంది.

STATE_8.title: Branch independence — one switch, others unaffected

scene_composition.text: A switch sits on branch two of this parallel circuit.
Open it — branch one keeps flowing, completely unaffected.
That is why every home wires its appliances in parallel.

tts_sentences.text_en: One switch sits on branch two: open it in parallel mode.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक switch branch two पर लगा है: उसे parallel mode में खोलिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక switch branch two మీద ఉంది: దాన్ని parallel mode లో తెరవండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Branch one keeps flowing at one ampere, completely unaffected — only branch two goes dark.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Branch one एक ampere पर बहता रहता है, बिल्कुल अप्रभावित — सिर्फ़ branch two अंधेरे में चला जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Branch one ఒక ampere వద్ద ప్రవహిస్తూనే ఉంటుంది, ఏమాత్రం ప్రభావితం కాకుండా — branch two మాత్రమే చీకటిలోకి వెళ్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Old series fairy lights are the opposite — one bulb dies and the whole string goes dark.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पुरानी series fairy lights इसके उलट होती हैं — एक bulb बंद होते ही पूरी string अंधेरी हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: పాత series fairy lights దీనికి వ్యతిరేకం — ఒక bulb ఆరిపోగానే మొత్తం string చీకటైపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is why every home wires its appliances in parallel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसीलिए हर घर अपने appliances को parallel में वायर करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అందుకే ప్రతి ఇల్లు తన appliances ని parallel లో వైర్ చేస్తుంది.

STATE_9.title: Explore — every dial is yours: V, R1, R2, topology, switch

scene_composition.text: Every dial is live: voltage, both resistors, topology, and the switch.
Every meter, the R_eq box, and the formula respond live as you drag.
Series: R_eq = R1+R2. Parallel: 1/R_eq = 1/R1+1/R2.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every dial is yours: voltage V, both resistors, series or parallel, and the switch — watch every meter respond live.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर dial अब आपका है: voltage V, दोनों resistors, series या parallel, और switch — हर meter को live respond करते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి dial ఇప్పుడు మీదే: voltage V, రెండు resistors, series లేదా parallel, మరియు switch — ప్రతి meter live గా స్పందించడం గమనించండి.

regeneration_variants.label: Farm irrigation channels — one long channel vs splitting into parallel channels
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A farmer's water channel: joining two narrow channels end-to-end (series) makes the water struggle further, but cutting a second channel alongside the first (parallel) gives the water two easy paths and it drains faster overall.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Same combination laws — channel length in series adds resistance to flow, while parallel channels each carry their own share and the combined resistance to flow drops below either channel alone.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Optional supplementary anchor; the primary teaching anchor stays the home switchboard / festival-lights pair.

regeneration_variants.label: City traffic — one lane vs two parallel lanes to the same destination
regeneration_variants.physical_world: Merging two lanes into one long queue (series) backs traffic up further, but opening a second parallel lane to the same destination lets more cars through per minute even though nothing about the first lane changed.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Same combination laws — series bottlenecks stack up resistance to flow, parallel lanes each carry their own share of traffic and the combined resistance to flow drops with every lane added.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Mirrors the primary home-switchboard anchor from a traffic-flow angle; optional supplementary anchor for STATE_5/STATE_6.

STATE_9.label: Explore: V, R1, R2, topology, switch
STATE_9.caption: All yours
